rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST U/W/O OF PAUL ZENTNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Guardian Ad Litem And Confirming Right To Decant Irrevocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/8/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The ex parte petition to appoint a guardian ad litem, while filed contemporaneously with this petition, was not presented to the Court in the manner provided in LRSF 14.11 B. It was just assigned on 2/28/23 to the examiner also assigned to review this petition. Drop-off ex parte petitions are usually reviewed in about a month from the date they are assigned to an examiner. Until a guardian ad litem is appointed, the relief sought in the underlying petition cannot be granted. (2) No proof of service of notice appears to have been filed for either petition. (3) The petition is a little lean on information, such as the date on which Nancy Rasch was appointed as the guardian ad litem (in the estate case, not this trust case), or what the terms of the "stipulation" were (or when, "previously", it was filed), or identifying who Matteo Zentner and Maya Zentner are or setting forth their ages... (4) Is there no conflict with a beneficiary representing other beneficiaries? (5) Elsa Balton's consent is not attached to this petition. Is she represented by an attorney? (6) What happened to Liam Balton, whose interest Ms. Rasch was also appointed to represent? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/27/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.])

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF AVA LOUISE VASQUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) As of January 2008, the Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all guardianship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575(e)(5).] (2) No explanation is given for why this accounting period ends on 10/1/22. (3) There is no liabilities schedule, although attorney fees authorized in the second accounting were reportedly not yet paid. (4) Among the disbursements were another $380 transferred to the guardian on 6/23/22 without any explanation. (The guardian is not making "gifts" to the estate for the minor to purchase personal items or food. It is a parent's responsibility to house, clothe, and feed her child. The guardianship estate should not be treated as a personal bank account; assets must be preserved for the minor.) (5) The SF Fire Credit Union account, however, is in the mother's name, individually, not this estate's, and appears to consist of two accounts, not just one as shown on Schedule F. Was the guardian authorized to retain the unblocked account in her own name? (6) The funds in the one blocked account exceed the FDIC amount. (7) Both bank statements end on 9/30/22; the accounting period ends on 10/1/22. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(c)(2).] (There is no need to file financial statements for all periods in an interim accounting; that is only required for the first accounting.] (8) Attorney fees incurred to defend the mother's actions in retaining the tuition refund should not be the burden of the guardianship estate. Also, it is unclear why the attorney had to personally travel to the Court on 10/5/21 to file bank records. Why could they not be sent via the USPS or a commercial courier service? (9) Attorney costs are not itemized to show what was filed or sent via messenger. The ones charged on 7/27/21 and 9/1/21 do not correspond to any filing with the Court on 6/8/21 and 7/7/21. After these issues are addressed (in a supplement signed and verified by the guardian as to the questions about her accounting and in a supplement to the fee declaration signed and verified by the attorney as to the questions about her fees and costs), an appearance will likely be required. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID WILLIAM BOWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing but with a slight correction in the attorney statutory fee, which should be $7,619.17, not $7,619.71, and because the administrator is the sole heir. The petition was reviewed online in hopes of not continuing the hearing; no courtesy copy was provided, as required by LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C. If there is $142,437.08 in cash on hand, it is unclear how the statutory attorney fee will be paid if that full amount is to be distributed to the heir. Paragraph 14 of the petition was meaningless, as there was no accounting. In any event, the petition did not provide all of the information required by CRC 7.550(b); the Court assumes the administrator liquidated the securities to arrive at just cash. Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN APPOINTED. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD GARLAND NOBLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/24/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues (last updated and e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 01/13/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the most recent Schwab account statement has not been filed. 2) The supplement filed on 10/12/2022 identified the 2000 Toyota as an asset of the estate proposed to be distributed to Catherine Noble, but the car has not been inventoried and appraised. If the car is not an asset of the estate, as so alleged in the petition, a copy of documentation showing how the decedent held title to the car must be filed. 3) The Cartier watch proposed to be distributed to the petitioner has not been inventoried and appraised. 4) 7% interest must be paid on the $3,000 cash gift to Catherine Noble, as required by Probate Code section 12003. The interest calculation must be provided, and the amount of the residual cash distributable to each beneficiary must be adjusted. 5) The Schwab account statement attached to the petition does not show 770 shares of Bank of America common stock inventoried and appraised.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK ALAN WINKLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, based on the sole heir's waiver of notice. Notice was defective, as it was mailed before the amended pleading was filed and still did not have the required language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6. and 14.14. A.] A petition for final distribution must set forth the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] An amended petition completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends; there is no need to state that the amended petiton corrects issues identified by the court. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE OSTBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if notice to the French consulate may be waived in order for this estate to finally proceed. Yet another Civil form of Proof of Service was filed on 2/3/23. The attorney declares that she mailed notice of the "foregoing" Notice of Petition to Administer Estate to the Consulate General of France on 2/3/23, but the notice attached to that proof of service was the one showing the hearing on 11/22/21, not this hearing. The Court might also wish to disregard part of the statement in paragraph 3 of the attorneyâs declaration filed on 1/25/22, which claims that "Form DE-111 6. should be checked." The proposed executor, likely intended to state that "item 5a(6) of the petition" should have been checked to indicate that the decedent was not survived by a child. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The DE-115 form on file erroneously identified the subject property (incorrect bank account number). 2) The petition prays for authority to transfer $28,599.74 from Freddie Patrick Williams, Jr.'s estate, but the two accountings filed in Freddie Patrick Williams, Jr.'s probate case reported $28,590.74 as liabilities to this estate. 3) The petition prays for authority to place the funds in this estate's Chase bank account 4958, but the Chase bank account previously reported by the petitioner after the sale of the real property was 8906. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH KIRN BROZELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Court Determination Of Entitlement On Will With Interlineation Before Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/1/23. (No earlier available date.) (1) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] A cursory review of the petition online indicates that it is incomplete. A verified supplement to the petition must be filed to provide the missing exhibit(s). (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) It appears that notice was not given to the surviving husband, who was listed in the petition as a party to receive notice. Deadline for delivering courtesy copies of the petition, supplement, and new proof of service to the assigned examiner is 4/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Probate Code Section 17000 et seq. deals with trusts.)

(4) A cursory review of the petition online shows that the petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8 of the petition.
